headline: State Islamic councils' RM4.2 billion wakaf assets lack centralized registry
context: Wakaf (endowment) assets managed by 14 state Islamic councils are estimated at RM4.2 billion but no centralized national registry exists to track or verify holdings.

cards.big: RM4.2 billion in wakaf assets across 14 states — no central registry exists
cards.sub: Religious endowments managed without consolidated tracking or verification

cards.big: Wakaf jurisdiction falls exclusively under state religious authorities
cards.sub: Federal government has no legal mechanism to mandate standardized wakaf management

cards.big: An estimated 12,000 hectares of wakaf land remain undeveloped or underutilised
cards.sub: Without proper registry, endowment assets cannot be efficiently managed or mobilised

cards.big: Turkey's Directorate of Foundations manages all wakaf through unified national system
cards.sub: Centralized management of religious endowments is practiced successfully elsewhere

cards.big: Wakaf donors gave for perpetual benefit — poor management betrays their religious intention
Should Malaysia create a national wakaf registry to protect and optimize endowment assets?
